//: Merged (not overwritten) into .claude/settings.json by the installer (pack.yaml: merge: true). Adds Node/TypeScript tooling permissions on top of the core file. The core settings.json carries no npm/npx entries at all (its allow-list is dotnet + git + gh), so every entry here is additive — nothing is restated. `deny` is deliberately absent: denying is core's job, and this stack introduces no new class of destructive command. NOTE ON `npx`: there is intentionally no broad `Bash(npx:*)` ask rule. Rules are evaluated deny -> ask -> allow and specificity does NOT change that order, so a blanket npx ask would out-rank the four specific npx allows below and prompt on every test run. It is also unnecessary: an npx invocation that matches no rule already prompts by default. The allows below are scoped to the four tools this pack's ci-profile actually declares, each of which npx resolves from the repo's own devDependencies (installed by `npm ci`) rather than fetching from the registry.
